name: Teradyne, Inc.
window: Q1 FY26 print → intra-quarter · Apr 29 → Jun 22, 2026

events.text: Q1 FY26 (quarter ended Mar 29) — a record. Revenue $1,282.5M, +87% y/y / +18% q/q ($200M above the prior 2021 peak); GAAP EPS $2.53, non-GAAP $2.56; non-GAAP GM 60.9% (+370bps q/q); record operating margin 37.5%. Semi Test $1,110.8M (+104.8% y/y; SoC $882M ~75% AI-compute, Memory $203M), Robotics $91.3M (+32% y/y), Product Test $80.4M. AI ~70% of revenue. Q2 guide: revenue $1.15-1.25B, non-GAAP EPS $1.86-2.15, GM 58-59%.

events.src: UBS · Arcuri / Shannon Poehlen / Gita Atalier
events.text: UBS-Teradyne deep-dive on CPO/silicon photonics: real optionality — four test insertions, mid-term TAM of $300-700M, first-mover via Fi-Con Tech + Quantifi/Photon 100. Ramp weighted to H2.
In the weekly preview, TER among the top picks at a higher ref: "Teradyne at 320... that's one I like a lot," a late-cycle laggard.

Preview: 2H improvement "nuanced positive" — stronger memory test demand → H2 upside; 1H still leads 2H on the VIP-compute split (~85/15 H1/H2). Google ASIC (VIP compute) qual finishing ~end-3Q, modest 4Q revenue and ramp in 2027; Intel merchant test shift is an out-year option. Read-through from Lumentum OCS outsourcing.
"Teradyne... talking about being qualified for Google TPU — that's pretty good stuff" — incremental SoC-test design-win color for the ASIC ramp.
TER on the test/semicap events calendar (corporate access).

events.src: AlphaSense Deep Research · LRCX primer (Mizuho comp)
events.text: TER at CY27E P/E 38.2x / EV/FCF 49.8x / P/Sales 10.9x (~$60bn mkt cap) — the richest EV/FCF in Mizuho's semicap comp table.

cells: SoC $440M (+11% q/q); test intensity rising with chiplets
Memory price/availability hurting demand outside iOS
~70% of revenue; three AI waves framed
